title: New Hampshire

jokes.text: Ben: Am I dreaming? Do I have my pants on?
jokes.setup: Overwhelming moment of Selina becoming president
jokes.punchline: Ben's non-sequitur question about his pants in this historic moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Ben's crude practicality amid historic gravity. Economy of language is excellent.

jokes.text: Ben: Ma'am, I don't wanna piss on your bliss, but...
jokes.punchline: Ben's crude alliteration mixed with formal address
jokes.explanation: Classic Veep wordplay - crude but clever alliteration that fits Ben's character perfectly.

jokes.text: Mike: (yells) President of the United States!
jokes.setup: Selina can't remember what she's supposed to do
jokes.punchline: Mike's explosive reminder of the obvious historic moment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke but fairly standard sitcom territory. Works for Mike's character.

jokes.text: Selina: Hey, you know what, go? We have to be careful, though, not to look too happy, you know? 'Cause of FLOTUS.
jokes.punchline: Selina's concern about appearing appropriately somber while clearly ecstatic
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's political calculation even in her moment of triumph. Good character work.

jokes.text: Amy: I'm not sure that Beyoncé fully understands that. / Selina: (gasps) She's calling me a 'Single Lady.'
jokes.punchline: Selina misinterpreting the song reference as political commentary
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference with Selina's characteristic self-centeredness.

jokes.text: Selina: Holy shit. It's like Lolla-fucking-palooza.
jokes.punchline: Selina's crude but enthusiastic comparison to music festival
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina profanity, though not particularly original. Fits character well.

jokes.text: Dan: It's like I'm a Beatle. / Staff: Well, you got the haircut for it.
jokes.setup: Dan comparing himself to a Beatle
jokes.punchline: Quick insult about his haircut
jokes.explanation: Clean setup-punchline structure. Basic but effective insult comedy.

jokes.text: Selina: Thank you so much, 'Senator Suck-up.'
jokes.punchline: Selina's immediate dismissive nickname
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina move - accepting praise while simultaneously insulting the giver.

jokes.text: Staff: Ma'am? You're now only a point away from Chung. Presidential bounce. / Selina: Yeah, bounce that, ya dumb dick.
jokes.setup: Staff member explaining presidential bounce in polls
jokes.punchline: Selina's crude wordplay on 'bounce'
jokes.explanation: Typical Selina wordplay with crude humor. Fits her character's competitive nature.

jokes.text: Dan: From Ashton Kutcher's assistant, 'Ashton says congrats.' / Selina: Assistant?
jokes.punchline: Deflating detail that it's from an assistant
jokes.explanation: Good deflation joke. Shows how even presidential congratulations can be hollow.

jokes.text: Ben: Goodbye, China.
jokes.punchline: Ben's casual comment about nuclear warfare
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy. Perfect economy - two words that encapsulate the terrifying reality of nuclear power in incompetent hands.

jokes.text: Selina: God, can't we just take 'em out? Is Jack Ruby still alive?
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to presidential assassination
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Selina's casual relationship with extreme solutions. Historical reference adds depth.

jokes.text: Selina: I wanna get rid of Leslie Carr. She has no fucking energy, ironically.
jokes.punchline: Ironic observation about Energy Secretary lacking energy
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay with irony. Sets up the later mix-up with Leslie Kerr.

jokes.text: Ben: Listen, let 'em sulk. I mean, who's gonna notice?
jokes.punchline: Ben's dismissive comment about Canada's irrelevance
jokes.explanation: Classic Ben dismissiveness. Good character consistency with his crude pragmatism.

jokes.text: Selina: Oh, my God. We left him in New Hampshire.
jokes.punchline: Realizing they abandoned their most devoted aide
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment. Shows how self-absorbed they all are that they'd forget Gary.

jokes.text: Selina: God, there's so many people in here. It's like a Mormon orgy.
jokes.setup: Overwhelmed by crowd in White House
jokes.punchline: Bizarre comparison to Mormon orgy
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd comparison. The juxtaposition of Mormon conservatism with orgy is perfectly Selina.

jokes.text: Ben: Here, boy. Come to me.
jokes.punchline: Ben responding like a dog
jokes.explanation: Shows Ben's willingness to debase himself. Good character moment.

jokes.text: Ben: I was bulimic the whole first year and I didn't even lose any weight from it.
jokes.setup: Ben refusing to be Chief of Staff again
jokes.punchline: Absurd detail about ineffective bulimia
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy. The mundane complaint about weight loss in the context of a serious eating disorder is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Selina: No, you weren't. None of that is true. What are you talking about?
jokes.punchline: Selina's matter-of-fact dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat. Selina's complete dismissal of Ben's dramatic claim is very her.

jokes.text: Selina: So, guess what? I am 10 centimeters dilated. I'm fully effaced. I mean, this presidency is crowning. I need you.
jokes.setup: Trying to convince Ben to be Chief of Staff
jokes.punchline: Extended childbirth metaphor for the presidency
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extended metaphor. The medical precision of the childbirth terms applied to politics is wonderfully absurd.

jokes.text: Selina: Ben, I swear to sweet Jesus Christ, if you don't do this, I'm gonna bring back Prohibition.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre threat to bring back Prohibition
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and bizarre threat. Shows Selina's tendency toward dramatic overreaction.

jokes.text: Male reporter: he's so Washington, his blood type is DC.
jokes.punchline: Blood type DC pun
jokes.explanation: Clean wordplay. Works as background media commentary humor.

jokes.text: Amy: Clue... it's me.
jokes.punchline: Her own answer delivered like a game show
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy moment - assertive and slightly condescending. Good economy.

jokes.text: Amy: Thank you for interrupting your studies, your start-ups, your retirements, your federal sentences.
jokes.punchline: Casual inclusion of 'federal sentences' in the list
jokes.explanation: Great escalating list joke. The casual inclusion of criminals among volunteers is perfectly cynical.

jokes.text: Staff: It's like a Google Maps of ass-kissing.
jokes.setup: International leaders wanting to call the president
jokes.punchline: Google Maps metaphor for diplomatic hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Modern, relatable metaphor for diplomatic protocol. Good visual imagery.

jokes.text: Mike: I'm not talking about this first female president thing. I'm talking about the first redhead press secretary.
jokes.punchline: Mike making it about himself and his hair color
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike moment - making a historic presidency about his hair. Great character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Mike: Uh, 'Mike McLintock, why are you so darn handsome?' / (press corps laughing) / Uh, why are you laughing at that one?
jokes.setup: Mike asking himself a question at press briefing
jokes.punchline: His hurt reaction when press laughs at his vanity
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy. Mike's vanity colliding with reality is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: Richard: 'Dynamic Duo' came in. That's Batman, Boy Wonder... young... Lady Wonder.
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt to include the female volunteer
jokes.explanation: Classic Richard awkwardness. His attempt at inclusivity makes it worse.

jokes.text: Richard: Yeah, I'll hit the phones until I have a sore throat and a hot ear.
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific physical consequences
jokes.explanation: Pure Richard - unnecessarily specific and slightly uncomfortable details.

jokes.text: Egan: I bring bad news to the closet.
jokes.punchline: Treating closet meetings as a normal practice
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan observation about the absurdity of government operations.

jokes.text: Egan: Give you 24 hours? Like in a movie? / Superior: No, I mean, yes. Please. / You have six hours. We can't afford 24.
jokes.setup: Dan asking for time to fix the problem
jokes.punchline: Meta reference to movie tropes, then immediate reduction of time
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor that acknowledges TV/movie conventions while subverting them.

jokes.text: Superior: Or for six hours, depending on how you do. / I'll see you then. / Alternatively, goodbye forever.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of permanent goodbye
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with deadpan delivery. The casual threat is well-executed.

jokes.text: Mike: My bit about gun control killed. My bit about voter ID laws identified itself as hilarious.
jokes.setup: Mike describing his press briefing success
jokes.punchline: Puns on 'killed' and 'identified'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Mike's pride in his performance. Good character moment.

jokes.text: Ben: You know, I told you when I was telling you I wasn't telling you what I told you. The fuck stops here, Dan.
jokes.setup: Dan claiming Ben told him about torture
jokes.punchline: Ben's convoluted denial and bastardization of 'buck stops here'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay and political reference. The convoluted logic is perfectly Ben, and 'fuck stops here' is quotable gold.

jokes.text: Jonah: So I'm gonna go to Africa and help install sewage systems in poor communities or whatever. Or go to Wall Street, I don't know. One of the two.
jokes.setup: Jonah claiming to be done with DC
jokes.punchline: Treating humanitarian work and Wall Street as equivalent alternatives
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah obliviousness. The casual equation of charity work and Wall Street shows his complete lack of moral compass.

jokes.text: Jonah: I've never told a lie in my life. / Dan: That was a lie. Try to keep up, Egan.
jokes.punchline: Dan's immediate logical correction
jokes.explanation: Classic logical contradiction joke with good character dynamics. Dan's condescension is perfect.

jokes.text: Jonah: I can't denounce it. That's like Peter denouncing Christ or worse.
jokes.punchline: Comparing internet loyalty to Peter's betrayal of Christ
jokes.explanation: Brilliant overstatement showing Jonah's warped priorities. The religious comparison is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Dan: Just to warn you, though... I do like to lick the rim.
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo about margarita glasses
jokes.explanation: Basic sexual humor that fits Dan's character but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Jonah: 'Three-pack-of-Kleenex' Rachel?
jokes.punchline: Crude nickname implying sexual activity
jokes.explanation: Crude but economical. Fits the characters' crude workplace culture but is fairly cheap humor.

jokes.text: Jonah: I couldn't be any closer to the Oval Office if I had... by some triumph of common sense... a desk actually in it.
jokes.punchline: Self-aware comment about his actual qualifications
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of Jonah self-awareness. The phrase 'triumph of common sense' is particularly good.

jokes.text: Selina: Oh, you... a million things. Are you kidding me? You know, just being there, and... and being you... (stammering) there... and all...
jokes.setup: Catherine asking what she's done to help
jokes.punchline: Selina's complete inability to answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment showing Selina's relationship with her daughter. The stammering makes it painfully real.

jokes.text: (Jingling during presidential oath)
jokes.punchline: Gary's keys jingling during the most important moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual gag. Gary's devotion creating disruption at the worst possible moment is quintessential Veep.

jokes.text: Jonah: There's one of me fuckin' a chicken while dressed as bin Laden. / Dan: The chicken is dressed as bin Laden or you're dressed as bin Laden? / Jonah: No, I'm dressed as bin Laden, Dan!
jokes.setup: Jonah describing internet memes about him
jokes.punchline: Dan's clarifying question about the costume logistics
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation. Dan's practical question about the meme logistics makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: Jonah: I'm like a boom op on a porn shoot right now.
jokes.setup: Complaining about being excluded from action
jokes.punchline: Specific analogy to porn film production
jokes.explanation: Surprisingly specific and accurate analogy. Shows Jonah's crude but occasionally insightful observations.

jokes.text: Dan: I can't have a terrorist chicken-fucker next to the president. Security risk.
jokes.punchline: Dan using the meme description as actual job qualification
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that treats the absurd meme as real security concern. Dan's practicality makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Mike: This is like Kathy Bates in 'Misery.'
jokes.setup: Gary forcing special shoes on Selina
jokes.punchline: Comparison to psychotic fan in horror movie
jokes.explanation: Apt pop culture reference that captures Gary's obsessive devotion perfectly.

jokes.text: Selina: Yeah, except there's no bridesmaids to fuck.
jokes.punchline: Selina's crude observation about wedding opportunities
jokes.explanation: Typical Selina crudeness but not particularly original. Fits character.

jokes.text: The squeaking shoes during the speech
jokes.punchline: Loud squeaking shoes destroying the dignity
jokes.explanation: Masterful physical comedy. The setup and payoff are perfect, and it destroys the most important moment of Selina's career.

jokes.text: Dan: It's like she's walking on a carpet of mice. / Jesus Christ, Gary. Are those real shoes or are those fucking dog toys?
jokes.punchline: Increasingly frustrated descriptions of the noise
jokes.explanation: Great escalating descriptions that enhance the visual gag. 'Carpet of mice' is particularly vivid.

jokes.text: Mike: Sounds like the theme from 'Psycho.'
jokes.punchline: Movie reference to horror film score
jokes.explanation: Perfect cultural reference that captures the ominous quality of the squeaking.

jokes.text: Staff: 'President squeaks to the nation.'
jokes.punchline: Pun on presidential address
jokes.explanation: Clean wordplay that shows how quickly media turns presidential moments into jokes.

jokes.text: Selina: Any fuckup from now on is not just a fuckup. It's my legacy.
jokes.punchline: Crude but accurate assessment of presidential responsibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina observation - crude language expressing genuine political insight.

jokes.text: Kent: You might as well drive a suicide clown car into the fucking Lincoln Memorial.
jokes.setup: Discussing how bad the PR would be
jokes.punchline: Incredibly specific and bizarre analogy
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant absurdist imagery. 'Suicide clown car' is peak Veep - specific, bizarre, and somehow perfectly appropriate.

jokes.text: Mike: Leslie Nielsen. Leslie Frazier, the coach for the Vikings... / Selina: Huh? / Mike: Doesn't matter. You're... good speech.
jokes.setup: Selina asking what man is named Leslie
jokes.punchline: Mike's helpful but irrelevant list, then giving up
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike moment - trying to be helpful when it doesn't matter at all.

jokes.text: Mike: I mean, we all make mistakes... just ask your mom and dad. (laughs)
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate personal insult to reporter
jokes.explanation: Classic inappropriate Mike moment. The nervous laugh makes it worse.

jokes.text: Mike: Otherwise I'd have to have you killed. (laughs)
jokes.punchline: Joking about assassination with nervous laugh
jokes.explanation: Inappropriate government spokesperson humor. Mike's nervous energy makes it cringe-worthy.

jokes.text: TV Ad: Imagine Selina Meyer is the only person keeping your children safe at night. 'Cause she is. Let's vote her out and make the Commander-in-Chief a Commander-in-Brief.
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'chief' and 'brief' suggesting incompetence
jokes.explanation: Clever political ad wordplay. Good pun structure but not character-specific.

jokes.text: Jonah: Well, then you better 'possible-ize' it, Dan.
jokes.punchline: Jonah's made-up word
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah wordplay - making up words while trying to sound authoritative.

jokes.text: Jonah: And henceforth they will no longer be known as 'Jonahs.' They'll be known as... Jimmys or Pepes or Sarahs or whatever the fuck that person's name turns out to be.
jokes.punchline: Refusing to let his name be used as generic term
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about Jonah's own status while showing his ego and awareness of being used.

jokes.text: Ben: Yeah, can we fly it into Danny Chung?
jokes.punchline: Ben's casual suggestion of assassination by aircraft
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben deadpan violence. The casual delivery makes the extreme suggestion funnier.

jokes.text: Dan: He's got old people coming out of his ass.
jokes.punchline: Crude but vivid imagery
jokes.explanation: Typical crude Dan expression. Gets the point across but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Selina: Yeah, everybody wants to fuck 'em, too. God, I would love to fuck a firefighter. Hey, I'm the president. I can fuck anybody I want now, right?
jokes.punchline: Selina's crude realization about presidential privileges
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina crudeness with insight about power. Shows her id completely unleashed.

jokes.text: Jonah: And that I will serve you faithfully as long as you happen to be president.
jokes.punchline: Conditional loyalty based on her continuing in office
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah moment - trying to be loyal while revealing his opportunistic nature.

jokes.text: Mike: She looks like she's in 'Flashdance.' / Kent: 'Thai lady-boy made head of sweatshop.'
jokes.punchline: Increasingly inappropriate comparisons
jokes.explanation: Escalating inappropriate media comparisons. Kent's line crosses into offensive territory typical of the show.

jokes.text: Mike: Our Lord Jesus started with 12. He didn't win New Hampshire, either.
jokes.setup: Trying to find positives in small vote count
jokes.punchline: Comparing Jesus to New Hampshire primary performance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant religious/political comparison. The specificity of New Hampshire makes it even better.

jokes.text: Selina: I came in third, Amy. Okay? Even the Nazis came in second.
jokes.setup: Amy trying to downplay third place finish
jokes.punchline: Comparing her performance to Nazi electoral success
jokes.explanation: Devastating historical comparison that perfectly captures Selina's self-pity. The specificity of 'second place' makes it brilliant.

tmdb_overview: Three days before the New Hampshire primary, Selina and her staff juggle her multiple official obligations and campaign appearances.
insight: Season finale deploys 66 jokes in 38 minutes—Veep's densest stretch yet.
